mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth signals robust consumer expenditure, which directly drives corporate earnings expansion and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If retail sales growth decelerates but S&P 500 still rises in >50% of occurrences, or if rank IC of retail sales growth vs 20-day returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate drops below 0.50%, monetary policy becomes extremely accommodative, compressing discount rates and boosting equity valuations. This threshold triggers a positive forward return signal over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If after this threshold is met, the 5-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of occurrences over a 1-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate signal a tightening labor market. This combination leads to wage pressures that compress corporate margins, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month increase > 0.2% and UNRATE month-over-month decrease > 0.1 percentage point
failure_condition: If in periods of simultaneous payroll increase and unemployment decline, forward equity returns are not significantly negative (t-stat < -1.96) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During industrial recessions defined by a 6-month cumulative decline in industrial production, a subsequent stabilization or rise in INDPRO predicts a strong rebound in equities as production recovers. The signal is conditional on the regime of industrial contraction.
failure_condition: If after the regime trigger, the 60-day forward return is not positive in at least 70% of historical cases, or if the mean return is not significantly greater than zero at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 600,000 units signal strength in residential investment, a leading indicator of broader economic expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST > 600 leads to negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of occurrences over any 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI coupled with no change in the federal funds rate indicates that real interest rates are falling, which is expansionary for equities. The joint condition of accelerating inflation and policy inaction predicts positive short-term returns.
regime_hint: lowflation regime with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: If the triggered condition yields negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of negative quarterly GDP growth (contraction), a subsequent positive GDP print signals the start of a recovery, leading to positive forward equity returns over the next 60 days. The regime is recession and the signal is the turnaround.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter change negative followed by positive
failure_condition: If in such recessionary turnarounds, the 60-day forward return is not positive in at least 75% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly above zero.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently high unemployment rate implies slack in the labor market, which reduces wage inflation and supports corporate profit margins. This structural condition is positive for equities over the medium term.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between UNRATE level and subsequent 20-day forward returns is negative over any 5-year rolling window, then reject.
